task_completion.description: Access Artifactory and log in as admin
task_completion.visual_evidence: Browser shows the Artifactory UI at http://localhost:8082, and there is an indication that the user is logged in as 'admin' (e.g., user profile icon or welcome message).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Artifacts repository browser
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Artifactory UI shows the 'Artifacts' section or repository tree view.

task_completion.description: Locate the target commons-lang3 version folder
task_completion.visual_evidence: The repository tree is expanded to show 'example-repo-local/org/apache/commons/commons-lang3/3.14.0'.

task_completion.description: Initiate deletion of the target folder or artifact
task_completion.visual_evidence: A delete action is triggered on the '3.14.0' folder or the 'commons-lang3-3.14.0.jar' file (e.g., right-click context menu showing 'Delete' or clicking a trash can icon).
A confirmation dialog for deleting the artifact/folder is shown and accepted, or a success toast notification appears confirming the deletion.

task_completion.description: Ensure the commons-io artifact is preserved
task_completion.visual_evidence: The repository tree still displays the 'commons-io' folder or the 'commons-io-2.15.1.jar' artifact under 'example-repo-local/org/apache/commons/', proving it was not deleted.

integrity.description: Agent used the web browser GUI to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Artifactory web interface in a browser, rather than using terminal commands (like curl) to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely interacted with the application's delete functionality
integrity.visual_evidence: The deletion is performed through the application's native buttons and dialogs, not by hiding elements using browser developer tools.

integrity.description: Agent did not use pre-written scripts to solve the task
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent does not open a terminal or text editor to write and execute a Python or Bash script to interact with the Artifactory API.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Artifactory URL: http://localhost:8082. Credentials: admin / password. Target artifact to delete: example-repo-local/org/apache/commons/commons-lang3/3.14.0/commons-lang3-3.14.0.jar (and its parent folder '3.14.0'). Artifact to preserve: example-repo-local/org/apache/commons/commons-io/2.15.1/commons-io-2.15.1.jar.
